quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 8
quiz.subtitle: Expert FTC with Complex Functions and Simplification (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus
quiz.topic: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus - Hardest Level

questions.question: Find \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^3} \frac{\sin t}{t^2} \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
Determine \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^3}^{x^2} \ln(1 + t^2) \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
Evaluate \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^4} \frac{t}{\sqrt{1 + t^4}} \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
Simplify \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{0}^{x} \left( \int_{0}^{t} \sin(u^2) \, du \right) dt\).
questions.explanation: Let \(F(t) = \int_{0}^{t} \sin(u^2) du\). Then the integral is \(\int_{0}^{x} F(t) dt\). By FTC Part 1, \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{0}^{x} F(t) dt = F(x) = \int_{0}^{x} \sin(u^2) du\).

questions.question: Find \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^3} \frac{\ln t}{t} \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
questions.explanation: By FTC: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(x^3)-F(x^2)] = f(x^3) \cdot 3x^2 - f(x^2) \cdot 2x = \frac{\ln(x^3)}{x^3} \cdot 3x^2 - \frac{\ln(x^2)}{x^2} \cdot 2x = \frac{3\ln x}{x^3} \cdot 3x^2 - \frac{2\ln x}{x^2} \cdot 2x = \frac{9\ln x}{x} - \frac{4\ln x}{x} = \frac{5\ln x}{x}\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{\sin x}^{\cos x} \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 - t^2}} \, dt\) for \(|x| < \frac{\pi}{2}\).
questions.explanation: By FTC: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(\cos x)-F(\sin x)] = f(\cos x) \cdot (-\sin x) - f(\sin x) \cdot \cos x = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\cos^2 x}} \cdot (-\sin x) - \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\sin^2 x}} \cdot \cos x\). For \(|x| < \frac{\pi}{2}\), \(\sin x > 0\) and \(\cos x > 0\), so \(\sqrt{1-\cos^2 x} = \sin x\) and \(\sqrt{1-\sin^2 x} = \cos x\). Thus \(= -\frac{\sin x}{\sin x} - \frac{\cos x}{\cos x} = -1 - 1 = -2\).

questions.question: Determine \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^3} \frac{1}{t \ln t} \, dt\) for \(x > 0, x \neq 1\).
questions.explanation: By FTC: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(x^3)-F(x^2)] = f(x^3) \cdot 3x^2 - f(x^2) \cdot 2x = \frac{1}{x^3 \ln(x^3)} \cdot 3x^2 - \frac{1}{x^2 \ln(x^2)} \cdot 2x = \frac{1}{x^3 \cdot 3\ln x} \cdot 3x^2 - \frac{1}{x^2 \cdot 2\ln x} \cdot 2x = \frac{3x^2}{3x^3 \ln x} - \frac{2x}{2x^2 \ln x} = \frac{1}{x \ln x} - \frac{1}{x \ln x} = 0\).

questions.question: Find \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^3} \frac{t^2}{1 + t^2} \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
questions.explanation: Note that \(f(t) = \frac{t^2}{1+t^2} = 1 - \frac{1}{1+t^2}\). By FTC: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(x^3)-F(x^2)] = f(x^3) \cdot 3x^2 - f(x^2) \cdot 2x = \left(1 - \frac{1}{1+x^6}\right) \cdot 3x^2 - \left(1 - \frac{1}{1+x^4}\right) \cdot 2x = 3x^2 - \frac{3x^2}{1+x^6} - 2x + \frac{2x}{1+x^4}\).

scoreMessages.90: Exceptional! You've mastered the most challenging FTC problems with complex functions and simplifications.
scoreMessages.70: Excellent! You handle advanced FTC applications with confidence. Review the ones you missed.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort! These are very challenging problems. Keep practicing with complex integrands and variable limits.
scoreMessages.0: Review FTC with both limits as functions, simplify carefully, and practice with nested integrals and trigonometric substitutions.
